name: scenario_Layers_composition
description: Multi-layer composition end-to-end: Layouts→Grid, TWO Layers under one Layers container (bottom Checkerboard, top Rainbow), Drivers→NetworkSendDriver. Proves the Drivers composite loop builds, allocates its output buffer, blends both enabled layers and feeds the result to the driver without crashing, and gates the bounded FPS so the N-pass composite cost is tracked. The exact alpha/additive blend math and the disable-drops-to-single-layer path are pinned by the unit tests (unit_BlendMap, unit_Layers_container); construct-mode set_control can't apply controls (built post-scheduler), so this scenario uses each Layer's default blend (alpha, full opacity) and asserts wired liveness + tick, not per-byte blend output.

steps.name: add-grid
steps.description: 128x128 GridLayout under Layouts (above host clock resolution so the composite tick is measurable).

steps.name: add-layers-group
steps.description: Top-level Layers container — the multi-layer composition host.

steps.name: add-bottom-layer
steps.description: Bottom Layer (composited first — clears + overwrites the output buffer). RGB.

steps.name: add-bottom-effect
steps.description: A Checkerboard base as the bottom layer's effect.

steps.name: add-top-layer
steps.description: Top Layer (composited second — blends onto the bottom with its default blend). RGB.

steps.name: add-top-effect
steps.description: Rainbow as the top layer's effect — composited over the Checkerboard base.

steps.name: add-driver-group
steps.description: Top-level Drivers container wired to the Layers container (composites all enabled layers into its output buffer).

steps.name: add-artnet
steps.description: Add NetworkSendDriver and run the bounded FPS measurement over the two-layer composite (min_pct gates on hardware; skipped with a WARN in the desktop runner).
